zh.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur是Tudor Ab Ieuan和Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel的兒子，出生。他至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur是Tudor Ab Ieuan和Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel的儿子，出生。他至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Tudur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Tudor
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Tudur, a derivative of British Celtic Toutorīx, literally ‘people’ + ‘king’. It has sometimes been taken as a Welsh equivalent of Latin Theodorus, from Greek Theodōros ‘gift of God’ (see Theodore ), for which the usual Welsh form is Tewdwr.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Tudor, a variant of Teodor (see Theodore ).

History: The Tudor royal family, English sovereigns from the accession of Henry VII in 1485 to the death of Elizabeth I in 1603, were of Welsh origin, being descended from Owain ap Maredudd ap Tudur, who held lands at Penmynydd in Anglesey.

nameMeanings.name: Ithel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: compound of iud ‘lord, prince’ + hael ‘generous’; borne in the Mabinogi by the son of Llarian.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur was born as the son of Tudor Ab Ieuan and Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel. He had at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur est né, fils de Tudor Ab Ieuan et Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel. Il a eu au moins 1 fille.

it.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur è nato il come figlio di Tudor Ab Ieuan e Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Ithel ap TudurはTudor Ab IeuanとMali "Bach" ferch Hywelの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur(은)는 Tudor Ab Ieuan(와)과 Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur nasceu filho de Tudor Ab Ieuan e Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur родился. Его родителей звали Tudor Ab Ieuan и Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Ithel ap Tudur nació como hijo de Tudor Ab Ieuan y Mali "Bach" ferch Hywel. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija.
